blocks.body: Tenses help you say when something happens. Start with Present Simple, Present Continuous, Past Simple, and Past Continuous, then move to Present Perfect and Present Perfect Continuous.

{"level":"A1–B1","intro":"Tenses help you show when something happens: regularly, now, in the past, in progress at a past moment, with a result now, or as a process up to the present moment.","rules":[{"label":"Time meaning","color":"emerald","text":"Choose a tense to show the time or kind of action.","example":"every day, now, yesterday, at 8 p.m. yesterday"},{"label":"Present Simple","color":"blue","text":"Start with the tense for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","example":"I work every day. / She lives in Warsaw."},{"label":"Present Continuous","color":"amber","text":"Then move to actions happening now or around the present time.","example":"I am studying now. / They are working this week."},{"label":"Past Simple","color":"rose","text":"Then add the tense for finished actions in the past.","example":"I visited my aunt yesterday. / We watched a film last night."},{"label":"Past Continuous","color":"violet","text":"After that, use the tense for an action that was in progress at a point in the past.","example":"I was studying at 8 p.m. / They were walking when it started to rain."},{"label":"Present Perfect","color":"cyan","text":"Then move to the tense for a result now, life experience, and time up to the present moment.","example":"I have lost my keys. / She has lived here for five years."},{"label":"Present Perfect Continuous","color":"teal","text":"Then add the tense for process, duration, and recent activity with a visible result.","example":"I have been studying for two hours. / She has been working all morning."}]}

{"title":"1. Starter block and next steps","intro":"Begin with four core tenses for A1–A2, then move to Present Perfect and Present Perfect Continuous.","items":[{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Present Simple","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and adverbs of frequency."},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Present Continuous","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and time expressions."},{"label":"A1–A2","title":"Past Simple","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and time expressions."},{"label":"A2","title":"Past Continuous","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and time expressions."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Present Perfect","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and time expressions with just, already, yet, ever, never, for, and since."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Present Perfect Continuous","subtitle":"Forms, negatives, questions, and time expressions with for, since, how long, lately, recently, and all day."}]}

{"title":"2. In short","items":["Tenses show when an action or state happens.","Present Simple gives you the base for habits, facts, and repeated actions.","Present Continuous adds actions happening now or around the present time.","Past Simple is used for finished actions and events in the past.","Past Continuous adds actions that were in progress at a moment in the past.","Present Perfect opens the next step: a result now, life experience, recent actions, and time up to the present.","Present Perfect Continuous adds process, duration up to now, and recent activity with a visible result.","The first four subsections build the starter base, and the pair Present Perfect + Present Perfect Continuous extends it further."]}
